description: Figure 5 presents the potentiodynamic polarization curves of Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C-xCu low-density steels with varying copper contents (0, 1, and 3 wt%) tested in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution at room temperature using a three-electrode system with SCE reference. The graph plots electrode potential (E, V vs SCE) against the logarithm of current density (log i, A/cm²), displaying three distinct curves: 0 Cu (black squares), 1 Cu (red circles), and 3 Cu (blue triangles). All alloys exhibit characteristic active-passive-transpassive behavior. The 0 Cu sample demonstrates the most positive corrosion potential (approximately -0.38 V vs SCE) and the widest passivation region, while increasing Cu content progressively shifts the pitting potential to more negative values and narrows the passive range. The corrosion current density increases with Cu addition, indicating that Cu alloying deteriorates the corrosion resistance despite refining carbide precipitates. This data supports the mechanism that Cu enrichment at grain boundaries creates microgalvanic couples with carbides, accelerating localized corrosion in chloride environments.
